channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: XBOX Reminds Gamers Modern Warfare 4 Isn’t Coming to Game Pass Yet
item.content:encoded:
XBOX has sent out notifications to Game Pass subscribers reminding them that Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 isn’t available on the service at launch, and a separate purchase is necessary.

Previous Call of Duty titles following Microsoft’s takeover of Activision Blizzard have been available day one with a Game Pass subscription, but that changes this year, and XBOX is reminding subscribers ahead of the November launch.

XBOX Reaffirms Modern Warfare 4’s Game Pass Omission at Launch

XBOX Game Pass subscribers have received messages reminding them of the changes to the service that come into effect with Modern Warfare 4, which won’t be included with a subscription at launch.

The message confirms that “Game Pass availability starts Holiday 2027” and that “if you’d like to jump in on day one, you’ll need to purchase the game,” followed by a button that opens Modern Warfare 4 on the Microsoft Store.

The changes to the Game Pass service were announced in April, when it was stated that, beginning with Modern Warfare 4, future Call of Duty titles won’t be available through Game Pass Ultimate or PC Game Pass at launch.

  • Related: XBOX CEO: ‘Call of Duty is Bigger Than the Marvel Cinematic Universe’

Instead, new Call of Duty titles will be added to the two services the following holiday season, about a year later, which, as Call of Duty fans will know, will be around the same time as the next launch in the series.

This means those who don’t splash out to purchase the latest Call of Duty title separately will remain one release behind in the cycle through Game Pass.

Check out the Modern Warfare 4 reveal trailer below.

Will you be buying Modern Warfare 4 at launch, or will you wait until the eventual Game Pass release? Let us know in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For your next read, check out the free update in Mario Kart World, and don’t forget to sign up for our Insider Gaming Newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Palworld 1.0 Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times
item.content:encoded:
After over two years in early access, Palworld’s big 1.0 release is right around the corner, and excitement levels are rising for the launch.

The creature-tamer from Pocketpair has endured a turbulent few years amid legal battles with Nintendo, but Palworld has emerged strong and still boasts one of the all-time highest concurrent player numbers on Steam.

Those numbers should spike again with the big 1.0 launch, and Insider Gaming has all the important details you need.

When is Palworld 1.0 Coming Out?

The full launch of Palworld and the big 1.0 release is set for Friday, July 10, 2026, and a swathe of changes is coming with the big update.

Developer Pocketpair has stated the 1.0 update comes with 27 pages’ worth of patch notes, and while the update won’t wipe existing save data, it’s advised that players start from scratch to get the full experience.

  • Related: Pocketpair Won’t Use AI in Games, Says Palworld Exec

Palworld’s big 1.0 launch comes after an extensive period in early access, which began in January 2024, and arrives after Pocketpair’s legal action from Nintendo.

It’s safe to say it’s been a turbulent few years for Palworld, but 2026 is a big one for the game, with the 1.0 launch coming alongside the release of an official TCG.

What Time Does Palworld 1.0 Release?

Pocketpair has not shared an official release time for Palworld’s 1.0 launch, though previous update times could provide an indicator.

As Pocketpair is based in Japan, the official release date of July 10 is set for that time zone, so the launch in some areas of the United States could actually come a day earlier on July 9.

  • Related: Nintendo’s Palworld Lawsuit Payout Might Be Tiny

Previous releases have come around 9 AM JST, which equates to 8 PM ET. However, at this point it’s just speculation, and we’ll have to wait and see when the exact time will be.

This article will be updated with the exact times when available.

Take a look at the Palworld 1.0 cinematic trailer below.

Are you excited for Palworld 1.0? Share your thoughts and what you’re looking forward to most in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For your next read, check out the free update in Mario Kart World, and don’t forget to sign up for our Insider Gaming Newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Free Mario Kart World Update Adds New Knockout Tour Routes & Photo Mode Features
item.content:encoded:
A new free update has been deployed for Mario Kart World, with version 1.7.0 adding new routes to Knockout Tour and a new feature that allows you to place stickers in Photo Mode.

Released on June 5, 2025, as a Nintendo Switch 2 exclusive launch title, Mario Kart World has received many free updates in the past year, and the latest adds new content to enjoy.

Mario Kart World Gets New Knockout Tour Routes

In a press release from Nintendo, the details of the two new Knockout Tour Routes were revealed, called Drill Rally and Boomerang Rally.

Drill Rally is a route connecting Wario Shipyard and Bowser’s Castle, while Boomerang Rally connects Salty Salty Speedway to Whistlestop Summit.

The routes become selectable once at least one Knockout Tour rally has been cleared, and it’s been confirmed that additional Knockout Tour routes are planned for future updates.

  • Related: New Nintendo Switch 2 With Updated Screen Could Fix Ghosting Issue

Additionally, the update now allows you to place stickers that you’ve collected when taking pictures using Photo Mode, which can be combined with frames.

Stickers can be placed with touch controls or Joy-Con 2 mouse controls, adding further customization options for those who want to create original photos.

While the new content will be appreciated by players, many are still hoping for DLC to become available for the game, which seems inevitable given the years of support that Mario Kart 8 received.

Check out the update video for Mario Kart 1.7.0 below.

What do you want to see added to Mario Kart World in future updates? Let us know via the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, read about a report claiming State of Decay 3 developer Undead Labs could be shut down, and be sure to sign up for our free Weekly Newsletter.

item.title: Tarkov Head Splits Community With New ‘Kord Breach’ Season Image
item.content:encoded:
Escape from Tarkov’s studio head, Nikita Buyanov, has revealed an image related to the upcoming Kord Breach season, the first official season in Tarkov’s history. It kickstarts a new initiative to introduce a fresh character angle, offering players highly modifiable ways to play with a model that resets every few months.

Kord Breach, which is the name of the first season, is based around the Black Division faction that was introduced in the ICEBREAKER update. In a new post on social media, Buyanov revealed some of the potential modifiers coming to the first season, and they’ve immediately split the community.

Tarkov’s First Season Draws Closer

This month, Escape from Tarkov will get an all-new way to play the game, with Battlestate Games adding seasonal characters with the Kord Breach update. These will be one-shot characters that exist for a season and then never again, subject to various modifiers, both global and personal, during a season that will include a battle pass, unique rewards, and a tougher gameplay loop.

The first thing that triggered some folks on social media was the revelation (again) that the seasonal character model is bound exclusively to the game’s PvP environment, which locks PvE players out of the opportunity to enjoy the new mode.

This isn’t a new piece of information, though. Nikita has long said that seasons will be PvP-exclusive… For now.

In the image Nikita posted on social media, we see some of the personal modifiers coming with the first season of Escape from Tarkov. These personal modifiers are what players can pick from to make their ride through the season easier or tougher, and there’s one buff in particular that has fans split down the middle.

It’s called Average, and it’s one of the most ‘expensive’ modifiers players can pick heading into Kord Breach. With this modifier, players have all their skills set to level 25 when the season starts, but they’ll never be able to grind them further. This is brilliant for the more casual, ’employed’ gamers, but it’s a tough one for those who want to crank certain skills to the max.

The negative modifiers coming with Kord Breach certain play true to their name. There’s one that locks player-to-player trading on the Flea Market, and another that makes players allergic to random medical or food items, without stating up front what they’ll be or what the effects of that allergy are.

  • RELATED: Tarkov Head Reveals Ancient Game Plans

Folks were even annoyed that some of the ‘toughest achievements in the game’, such as unlocking the Kappa safe container, will now be instantly available simply by ticking a box at the start of the season. It’s the most expensive modifier in the game, but ‘Kappa Protocol’ gives you the game’s largest safe container from the outset.

Are you torn by any of the modifiers lined up for Kord Breach in Escape from Tarkov? It’s worth stating that the screenshot provided is scrolled to the bottom, so there are many more modifiers to pick from. Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Terminal War has sumptuous new screenshots, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Pokémon TCG 30th Celebration: Release Date & Pre-Order Guide
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon is going all out in its 30th anniversary year, and after months of celebration in other areas, the festival finally comes to the TCG with the 30th Celebration set.

Hype for the 30th Celebration TCG set is reaching unprecedented levels, which could make it hard to get your hands on products, especially with the plague of scalpers in the hobby. Still, the hope is that the Celebration set will be printed at a high rate.

For all the details on the full product line-up of the 30th Celebration set, Insider Gaming has you covered.

What is the Pokémon TCG 30th Celebration Release Date?

The release date for the Pokémon TCG 30th Anniversary Celebration is September 16, 2026, worldwide, but in the English markets, there are several product drops.

On the initial launch, a wealth of products will be available, but some of the larger products, including the highly anticipated Ultra Premium Collections, will be released at a later date.

  • Related: Pokémon 30th Anniversary Classic Collection Cards Are Not Exclusive to UPC

In total, seven products are released on September 16, three on October 2nd, one on October 30, and the final three in the line-up are available from November 6.

All Pokémon TCG 30th Celebration Products

The full product range of Pokémon 30th Celebration Products includes 14 separate products, many of which contain exclusive promos.

That will make for an expensive outlay for anyone pursuing a full master set of the 30th Celebration line, including all promos, particularly with four locked behind two Ultra Premium Collections.

The table below shows all of the upcoming products in the 30th Anniversary set, the release date, and what’s included.

Check out the reveal trailer for the 30th anniversary set below.

Are you looking forward to the Pokémon 30th Anniversary TCG set? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon, find out how to make a Surrounded by Stars Habitat in Pokopi

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: ‘Last of Us Multiplayer’ Game Terminal War Reveals Apocalyptic Visuals in New Shots
item.content:encoded:
If you were lucky enough to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase at the end of May, you’ll have seen a new trailer from Terminal War, an upcoming third-person shooter that’s deeply reminiscent of the canceled Last of Us multiplayer title. It’s an homage to Factions, the competitive portion of the first Last of Us title that dropped in 2013, and it is a hugely anticipated shooter.

In a new post on social media, the team at Albatross Interactive, the studio behind Terminal War, revealed two new screenshots of the game, comparing lighting effects on an unnamed map, making prospective fans even more excited.

Terminal War Looks Incredible in Latest Images

In a post on X, the team at Albatross Interactive uncovered two new screenshots of Terminal War, showcasing lighting effects on a new, unnamed map. The environment could genuinely have been pulled straight from The Last of Us, Naughty Dog’s post-apocalyptic shooter that debuted in 2013, and the series that heavily inspired the original of Terminal War.

Those already excited about Terminal War wasted no time in hopping into the comments to participate in the debate. The split went right down the middle, with some preferring the color palette on the right because it boasts more of an ‘apocalyptic’ feel. Others felt like the left image was better because it was free of the ‘piss filter’ that covered so many games all those years ago.

In the comments, the team at Albatross also revealed some tidbits about the game, including the plan to have unique weather and lighting effects on every map in the game, which is a 4v4 multiplayer shooter.

They also confirmed that the game might get a mobile release, but if it does, it’ll be much further down the line. First up, it’s coming to PC via early access, and then console releases are planned a little later, all being well.

Do you like the look of Terminal War? Let us know what you’re thinking about the game on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that XBOX isn’t planning to cut spending on games, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Pokémon 30th Anniversary Classic Collection Cards Are Not Exclusive to UPC
item.content:encoded:
Confusion has spread across social media after the reveal of the full range of the Pokémon 30th Anniversary TCG set, with odd wording from The Pokémon Company leading some to incorrectly assume the reprinted cards in the Classic Collection are exclusive to the Ultra Premium Collections.

Two UPCs will be released in November as part of the Pokémon 30th Anniversary set, themed around day and night, which will include a pack containing three cards from the Classic Collection.

These packs are not available in any other products, which led many to incorrectly assume the Classic Collection cards would only be available through the UPCs. However, that is not the case.

30th Anniversary Classic Collection Cards Available in All Booster Packs

In the official press pack for the Pokémon 30th Anniversary TCG set, it’s stated that classic cards are included in the Pokémon TCG: 30th Celebration booster packs, though they will not be legal for play in the standard TCG format.

As previously announced, each booster pack will contain five foil cards, one of which will be one of the 30 Pikachu artworks in the set, one foil Basic Energy, and one Pokémon TCG Live code card.

  • Related: How to Get Sparkling Wish Notes in Pokémon Pokopia

Confirmation that the Classic Collection cards are in the main set will be a huge boost for collectors, who had feared having to splash out for UPCs to pull the cards in the subset.

However, the pack inside the UPC is merely a guaranteed way to obtain three of the Classic Collection reprinted cards, and is not the only way to obtain them.

Check out the reveal trailer for the 30th anniversary set below.

Are you looking forward to the Pokémon 30th Anniversary TCG set? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon, find out how to make a Surrounded by Stars Habitat in Pokopia, and don’t forget to sign up for the Insider Gaming newsletter.
